content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 29, 1988


Marcia C. Livingstone
160 Third Street
Woodland, CA 95804

Re: Your Request For Advice
    Our File No. I-88-237

Dear Ms. Livingstone:

     You have requested advice about application of conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1/
to your duties on the Yolo County Mental Health Advisory Board.

     Your advice request does not refer to a specific
governmental decision. Therefore, we are treating your
question as a request for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c)(3)(D) and (F) (copy enclosed).2/

QUESTION

     You are an unpaid member of the board of directors of the
nonprofit Yolo Community Care Continuum (Community Care). You
also are a board member of the Yolo County Mental Health
Advisory Board (Mental Health Advisory Board). Does your

____________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Marcia C. Livingstone
July 29, 1988
Page -2-


membership on the board of Community Care require that you
disqualify yourself from participating in decisions before the
Mental Health Advisory Board which concern Community Care?

CONCLUSION

Because Community Care is a nonprofit organization that is
not a source of income to you, your volunteer service on the
Community Care board does not create an economic interest in
the organization. Therefore, you may participate in decisions
concerning Community Care, which are before the Mental Health
Advisory Board.

FACTS

You are a member of the Board of Directors of the Mental
Health Advisory Board. You also are an unpaid board member of
Community Care. Community Care is a nonprofit (Short-Doyle)
contract agency. You are not an employee of Community Care nor
do you have a business interest in the organization, but you
are a client of Community Care.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making, or in any way attempting to influence
a governmental decision in which an official knows or has
reason to know he has a financial interest. An official has a
financial interest in a decision that will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, different from the
effect on the general public, on, among others, the following
economic interests:

     c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution
in the regular course of business on terms available
to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more
in value provided to, received by or promised to the
public official within 12 months prior to the time
when the decision is made.

     d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

Section 87103 (c) and (d).

Marcia C. Livingstone
July 29, 1988
Page -3-


     Community Care is not a source of income to you.  Your
services as a director are on a volunteer basis.  Your
contracting to receive health care services from Community
Care also does not provide income to you.  Therefore,
Section 87103(c) does not apply to your situation.

     For purposes of the Act, a business entity is an
organization or enterprise operated for profit.  (Section
82005.)  Community Care is a nonprofit organization, not a
business entity.  Your membership on the board of
Community Care does not make you an officer of a business
entity.  For this reason, Section 87103(d) does not apply
to your situation.

     Consequently, you may participate in decisions before
the Mental Health Advisory Board which concern Community
Care.

     I hope this letter satisfactorily answers your request
for advice.  Please call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have
any questions about this letter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Margarita Altamirano
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:MA:aa

Enclosure

JUN 24 2 09 1" 68

June 20, 1988

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

To Whom It May Concern:

I am a member of the Yolo County Mental Health Advisory Board
(MHAB), and also a member of the Board of the Yolo Community Care
Continuum (YCCC), a non-profit (Short-Doyle) contract agency. I
am also a consumer of YCCC. I am not an employee of YCCC, nor do
I have any business interest in YCCC.

I would like to know if there are specific current conflict of
interest regulations governing my participation on the Yolo
County MHAB.

Thank you for any assistance you can provide.

Sincerely,

Marcia Livingstone
Marcia C. Livingstone
160 Third Street
Woodland, CA 95695
